name: Maspeth Federal Savings And Loan Association
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank operating 8 branches in Queens and Nassau County, NY, offering fee-free checking, savings, mobile banking, and commercial lending.
description_long:
Maspeth Federal Savings And Loan Association is a federally insured community bank headquartered in Maspeth, Queens, with roots in serving local neighborhoods across Queens and Nassau County. The bank operates eight physical branches (Main Office in Maspeth, Loan Center, Astoria, Rego Park, Ridgewood, Fresh Meadows, Forest Hills, and New Hyde Park) and maintains FDIC insurance backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government.

The bank's core offerings include fee-free checking and savings accounts, with particular emphasis on their Perks Checking account that provides discounts at participating retailers and pharmacies. Additional services include online and mobile banking with mobile check deposits, commercial real estate lending, mortgage products, free ATM access, bank-by-mail services, money orders, wire transfers, and financial literacy programs for high school and college students. They advertise a focus on forward-thinking solutions while maintaining traditional community banking values.

Maspeth Federal distinguishes itself through explicit positioning as a community-focused institution. The bank sponsors dozens of community events annually, including a free summer concert series and "Smile on Maspeth Day." Their marketing emphasizes personal relationships and local knowledge, with customer testimonials highlighting employee knowledge and welcoming service. The mobile banking platform is highlighted as a key differentiator for convenience-focused customers.

As a traditional community bank with limited geographic footprint, Maspeth Federal is best suited for customers in the Queens and Nassau County area seeking relationship-based banking without monthly fees. The website provides adequate basic information, though details on interest rates, loan terms, and specific product features require visiting branches or calling directly. The bank's emphasis on community engagement and fee-free products suggests strong positioning for local customers, though online-only users seeking comparison shopping may find limited rate transparency. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Maspeth Perks Checking with retail and pharmacy discounts
Mobile banking app with mobile check deposit
Online account management and fund transfers
pros: Completely fee-free checking and savings accounts with no monthly maintenance charges
FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government guarantee
Maspeth Perks Checking provides discounts at participating retailers and pharmacies
Mobile banking with mobile check deposit capability and fund transfers
Eight physical branch locations across Queens and Nassau County for in-person service
Free ATM access with surcharge-free ATM network
Active community involvement with sponsored events and financial education programs
Commercial real estate lending available for business customers
cons: Limited geographic footprint (Queens and Nassau County only) makes it inaccessible for customers outside service area
No interest rates displayed on website; customers must contact branches directly for rate comparison shopping
Requires valid social security number to use mobile banking, which excludes some consumer segments
No mention of competitive CD rates or high-yield savings products compared to online banks
Limited product details on website; significant information gaps on loan terms, APRs, and account features
best_for: Queens and Nassau County residents seeking relationship-based community banking
Small business owners needing commercial real estate financing and local lending relationships
Customers prioritizing fee-free accounts and avoiding monthly maintenance charges
Students and young adults enrolled in their financial literacy programs
diagnosis: Maspeth Federal is ideal for consumers in Queens and Nassau County seeking traditional community banking with zero monthly fees and personalized service. The main caveat is geographic limitation—this bank is only suitable for customers within their service area, and online shoppers comparing rates will find insufficient pricing transparency on their website.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
fdic_official_name: Maspeth Federal Savings and Loan Association
